quick_skim.title: Contrastive Reflection for Iterative Prompt Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: LLM agents are becoming central to information retrieval: they issue retrieval queries, synthesize answers, and increasingly serve as judges for IR evaluation. Improving the prompts that control these agents is an optimization problem, but in applied IR settings it often looks less like blind search and more like debugging. Engineers need to know which behavior failed, which nearby behavior still worked, what distinguishes the two, and whether a prompt edit improves held-out quality without introducing regressions.   We present Contrastive Reflection, an iterative prompt-optimization framework for agentic IR workflows. The framework starts from a task-centric quality definition: QA agents expose retrieval or reasoning traces, and grading agents expose dimension-level scores and rationales. These structured traces are used to identify error-anchored behavioral slices, add nearby successful examples from the same region, and ask a Teacher LLM to propose a targeted prompt edit. Candidate edits are accepted only when validation performance improves, optionally subject to regression checks. We instantiate the framework with a tree-based slice selector, but the contribution is the contrastive reflection loop rather than the tree itself.   On a public HotpotQA retrieval-augmented QA setup, one tree-selected contrastive repair improves held-out exact-match accuracy from 51.4% to 60.4%. Failure-only and random-evidence variants improve less and break more previously correct examples. A light instruction-only comparison places the method near modern prompt optimizers: MIPROv2 reaches 59.4% and GEPA 57.0%. The result is an interpretable optimization loop for IR agents, aimed at making prompt repair more inspectable and validation-driven.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: iterative prompt optimization algorithm for LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM智能体的迭代提示优化算法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于LLM智能体的迭代提示优化算法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM智能体的迭代提示优化算法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Contrastive Reflection iteratively optimizes prompts for LLM agents by contrasting successful and failed behaviors, improving IR and QA performance without regressions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对LLM代理的提示优化问题，提出对比反思框架。该框架利用任务中心的质量定义，通过对比成功与失败行为来迭代改进提示。在信息检索和问答场景中，该方法有效提升了代理的性能，同时避免回归，为提示调试提供了系统化工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对LLM代理的提示优化问题，提出对比反思框架。该框架利用任务中心的质量定义，通过对比成功与失败行为来迭代改进提示。在信息检索和问答场景中，该方法有效提升了代理的性能，同时避免回归，为提示调试提供了系统化工具。

quick_skim.title: Modification-Considering Value Learning for Reward Hacking Mitigation in RL
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning agents can exploit misspecified reward signals to achieve high apparent returns while failing on the intended objective, a failure mode known as reward hacking. Existing practical defenses typically constrain policy updates to stay near a known safe reference, creating a tension between suppressing hacking and permitting legitimate improvement. We propose Modification-Considering Value Learning (MCVL), which operationalizes the theoretical idea of current utility optimization for standard value-based RL. MCVL wraps an off-policy learner and treats each incoming transition as a candidate modification: it forecasts two training paths, one that includes the transition and one that does not, and scores both with a frozen bootstrapped-return estimator derived from a learned reward model and value function. The transition is admitted only if inclusion does not decrease the score. We formalize conditions under which this filtering is both safe and permissive, and instantiate MCVL with DDQN and TD3. Across four safety-relevant gridworlds and three modified MuJoCo continuous-control tasks with diverse hacking mechanisms, MCVL mitigates reward hacking while continuing to improve the intended objective. Project website: ktolnos.github.io/mcvl/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Mitigates reward hacking via modification-considering value learning, relevant to reward modeling in RLHF
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过考虑修改的价值学习缓解奖励黑客，与RLHF中的奖励建模相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过考虑修改的价值学习缓解奖励黑客，与RLHF中的奖励建模相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过考虑修改的价值学习缓解奖励黑客，与RLHF中的奖励建模相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MCVL mitigates reward hacking in RL by treating each transition as a modification, forecasting two training paths, and using a frozen bootstrapped network to score them, improving robustness against misspecified rewards.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出考虑修改的价值学习（MCVL）方法，用于缓解强化学习中的奖励黑客问题。该方法将每个转移视为候选修改，分别预测包含和不包含该转移的两条训练路径，并使用冻结的引导网络打分。实验表明，MCVL在抑制奖励黑客的同时允许合理的策略改进，为奖励学习中的不确定性处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出考虑修改的价值学习（MCVL）方法，用于缓解强化学习中的奖励黑客问题。该方法将每个转移视为候选修改，分别预测包含和不包含该转移的两条训练路径，并使用冻结的引导网络打分。实验表明，MCVL在抑制奖励黑客的同时允许合理的策略改进，为奖励学习中的不确定性处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: reward modeling for learning human preferences in reinforcement learning from human feedback

quick_skim.title: Reliability-Prioritized Fine-Grained Generation in Multimodal Large
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly expected to generate fine-grained descriptions of visual content. However, we observe and theoretically show that generating fine-grained responses poses a reliability challenge, \textit{i.e.}, fine-grained generation is more error-prone than coarse-grained generation. This phenomenon suggests that models should generate the finest description that remains reliable rather than simply produce more specific outputs. To investigate this problem, we develop \textsc{GranFact}, a granularity-aware benchmark consisting of expert-verified multi-object images with coarse-to-fine category annotations. Then, we design a hierarchy-aware evaluation algorithm, which assesses both whether model predictions are visually correct and how specific the correct predictions are. We also propose a reliability-prioritized preference optimization method based on Direct Preference Optimization, which penalizes unreliable fine-grained claims while rewarding reliable specificity. Experiments on \textsc{GranFact} show that our method improves fine-grained generation while preserving reliability. Code and data are available \href{https://github.com/WeiWu2025/GranFact}{here}.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: addresses reliability in fine-grained generation, related to hallucination suppression in multimodal LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 关注细粒度生成的可靠性问题，与多模态大模型幻觉抑制相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 关注细粒度生成的可靠性问题，与多模态大模型幻觉抑制相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 关注细粒度生成的可靠性问题，与多模态大模型幻觉抑制相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reliability-Prioritized Fine-Grained Generation shows fine-grained MLLM outputs are more error-prone and proposes granularity-aware benchmark and evaluation to mitigate hallucination.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型生成细粒度描述时，越精细越容易出错，这类似于幻觉问题。本文开发GranFact基准和层次感知评估算法，引导模型在可靠性优先原则下生成最细但仍可靠的描述。方法通过从粗到细的类别注释和评估，有效降低了细粒度生成中的错误率，为多模态幻觉抑制提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态大模型生成细粒度描述时，越精细越容易出错，这类似于幻觉问题。本文开发GranFact基准和层次感知评估算法，引导模型在可靠性优先原则下生成最细但仍可靠的描述。方法通过从粗到细的类别注释和评估，有效降低了细粒度生成中的错误率，为多模态幻觉抑制提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: CADENZA in Action: Breaking the Monolith with Intent-Dependent Plan Spaces for Semantic Queries
quick_skim.abstract: Semantic query processing engines execute semantic operators, whose behavior is specified by natural-language intents, via model inference over multimodal data. Most existing optimizers optimize the operators at the granularity of monolithic implementations -- such as LLMs and embedding models -- forcing a trade-off between expensive model calls and cheaper alternatives that fail to capture intent-dependent semantics. We present CADENZA, a semantic operator optimizer that compiles an intent into decomposed steps, selects concrete physical implementations for each step, and tunes their parameters under user-specified quality-latency-cost preferences. In this demonstration, users interact with CADENZA through a web interface over multimodal databases, exploring how an intent is decomposed into alternative plans, how each plan is optimized, and how different preferences yield different winning plans.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimizes LLM-based semantic query operators with algorithm improvements
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过算法改进优化基于LLM的语义查询算子
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过算法改进优化基于LLM的语义查询算子
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过算法改进优化基于LLM的语义查询算子
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CADENZA decomposes intents into steps and selects optimal physical implementations for semantic queries, balancing quality, latency, and cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对语义查询引擎中LLM调用昂贵且替换方案缺乏语义的问题，提出CADENZA优化器。它将自然语言意图分解为多个步骤，为每步选择具体的物理实现并调参，在质量、延迟和成本间权衡。演示表明，CADENZA能有效打破单一实现限制，提升查询效率和准确性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对语义查询引擎中LLM调用昂贵且替换方案缺乏语义的问题，提出CADENZA优化器。它将自然语言意图分解为多个步骤，为每步选择具体的物理实现并调参，在质量、延迟和成本间权衡。演示表明，CADENZA能有效打破单一实现限制，提升查询效率和准确性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型后训练, especially work that connects or combines: hallucination suppression; l; p; r; o; reward modeling for learning human preferences in reinforcement learning from human feedback; innovation; math formulation; Algorithm improvements; servey.
